name: New Jersey Turnpike Authority
tagline: The toll-revenue-backed authority that runs the NJ Turnpike and Garden State Parkway — funding a multibillion-dollar capital program plus a long-running tolling, ITS, OT, and enterprise-IT modernization wave.
overview: The New Jersey Turnpike Authority (NJTA) is an independent state authority that operates and maintains two of the nation's busiest toll roads — the New Jersey Turnpike and the Garden State Parkway — from its Woodbridge headquarters. It is funded almost entirely by toll revenue rather than general appropriations, which gives it an unusually large and self-directed capital program: NJTA's board has authorized a multi-year capital plan reported at roughly $24B for the Turnpike and Parkway, financed in part by scheduled toll increases (a 3% hike took effect January 1, 2026). Beyond roadway and bridge construction, the Authority runs a deep technology footprint — E-ZPass and cashless-tolling systems, intelligent transportation systems (ITS) and traffic management, operational technology (OT) for field devices and SCADA-style controls, and enterprise IT and cybersecurity across its workforce and customer-service operations. For a Dell SLG seller, NJTA is a large, capital-rich, self-funded buyer whose tolling-modernization, data-center, OT/IT-convergence, and cyber needs run on its own public bidding process rather than a state co-op.

budget.intro: NJTA is toll-funded, not appropriation-funded, so its spending power tracks traffic and toll policy rather than the state budget. Its 2026 budget is reported at roughly $2.8B, with toll revenue expected to supply more than $2.3B of it; alongside that operating budget sits a multi-year capital program reported in the $24B range for the Turnpike and Parkway.

stats.label: 2026 budget
stats.note: Covers operations, maintenance, debt service, and reserves

stats.label: Toll revenue (2026)
stats.note: Primary funding source; 3% toll increase effective Jan 1, 2026

stats.label: Capital program
stats.note: Multi-year Turnpike + Parkway plan, toll-revenue and bond backed

notes: NJTA is self-funded through tolls and bonds — it does not buy through a state appropriation cycle, so timing follows its own board calendar and bond issuances.
The Authority also provides large dedicated transfers to NJ TRANSIT, a recurring claim on toll revenue to keep in view when sizing discretionary IT spend.

initiatives.name: E-ZPass back-office modernization
initiatives.summary: In September 2024 NJTA awarded a long-term contract (reported at ~$1.73B over 13 years) to TransCore for a new E-ZPass back-office account-management system and enhanced customer-service operations. A large, multi-year tolling-platform refresh with surrounding data, integration, and infrastructure needs.

initiatives.name: Cashless / all-electronic tolling
initiatives.summary: New Jersey toll roads are moving toward all-electronic tolling (the neighboring Atlantic City Expressway went fully cashless in January 2026). Removing toll plazas in favor of gantries and back-office processing shifts spend toward sensors, networking, edge/OT, and data systems.

initiatives.name: ITS & traffic management
initiatives.summary: Intelligent transportation systems — variable message signs, cameras, detection, and traffic operations centers — are an ongoing modernization track tied to the capital program. Edge compute, networking, storage, and analytics are natural infrastructure plays.

initiatives.name: Cybersecurity & OT/IT convergence
initiatives.summary: NJTA's IT organization has prioritized hardening cybersecurity across enterprise IT and the operational-technology environment that runs tolling and field systems. OT/IT convergence, segmentation, and resilient infrastructure are active needs as tolling goes fully digital.

procurement.intro: NJTA buys through its own public bidding and RFP process, not a state cooperative — vendors register with the Authority and respond to advertised solicitations. Two things to confirm early: that Dell is a registered NJTA vendor, and which contracts run direct versus through a reseller or any applicable state cooperative.

vehicles.name: NJTA public bidding / RFPs
vehicles.detail: The primary path. The Authority advertises goods and non-engineering-services opportunities (generally above the ~$53K threshold) on its site and in NJ newspapers; contracts above $100K require Board of Commissioners authorization.

vehicles.name: Goods & Non-Engineering Services
vehicles.detail: NJTA's procurement track for IT hardware, software, and services — registered vendors respond to RFPs scored on weighted criteria including price.

vehicles.name: State cooperative (where applicable)
vehicles.detail: For some commodity IT, New Jersey state cooperative/contract vehicles may apply alongside NJTA's own bidding — confirm eligibility per purchase rather than assuming co-op access.

powerStructure.name: Kris Kolluri, Esq.
powerStructure.title: Executive Director, NJTA
powerStructure.note: Leads the Authority (took the role in early 2026) while also serving as NJ TRANSIT CEO — the top executive relationship and a link between the two agencies' tech agendas.

powerStructure.name: Jose Dios
powerStructure.title: Chief Information Officer
powerStructure.note: Owns enterprise IT and cybersecurity direction — the primary technology buying-center relationship for Dell.

powerStructure.name: Erwin Luna
powerStructure.title: Chief Financial Officer
powerStructure.note: Owns the toll-funded budget and capital financing — key for sizing and timing of large IT commitments.

powerStructure.name: Angela McNally
powerStructure.title: Director of Procurement & Materials Management
powerStructure.note: Runs the public bidding and RFP process — the path onto NJTA contracts and vendor onboarding.

powerStructure.name: Priya Jain
powerStructure.title: Chair, Board of Commissioners
powerStructure.note: The governor-appointed board authorizes contracts above $100K, so board approval is a required gate for large deals.

news.title: NJTA moves procurement on ~$6.7B Newark Bay (Casciano) Bridge replacement
news.impact: The Authority is pre-qualifying contractors this summer for a toll-funded, four-lane cable-stayed replacement of the Vincent R. Casciano Memorial Bridge under the Newark Bay–Hudson County Extension Program (Gov. Sherrill scaled the original ~$10.7B twin-span down to ~$6.7B, targeting 2031). A signal of sustained capital spend that pulls surrounding ITS, OT, networking, and program-controls demand.

news.title: NJTA selects AECOM for 5-year general consulting engineering master services agreement
news.impact: A five-year MSA covering capital improvements, operations, and long-range planning — with explicit tolling/digital-services and program-controls scope. Confirms the modernization program has a lead consulting partner shaping technology-adjacent requirements.

news.title: NJTA solicits ERP business-administration software managed services (RFP RM-196137)
news.impact: An enterprise-software/managed-services RFP (submittals due May 11, 2026) — a direct enterprise-IT modernization signal and an entry point for infrastructure, data, and services conversations around the ERP platform.

news.title: Kris Kolluri takes over as NJTA Executive Director
news.impact: New top leadership (and shared leadership with NJ TRANSIT under Gov. Sherrill) — reset the executive relationship map and watch for aligned tech priorities across both agencies.

news.title: 3% toll increase takes effect on Turnpike and Parkway
news.impact: Reinforces the toll-revenue base funding the ~$2.8B budget and the multi-year capital program — supports continued IT and tolling investment.

news.title: NJTA board approves 2026 budget tied to toll-revenue plan
news.impact: Confirms operating and capital funding for the year — a window to align proposals to the approved spend.

dellActions: Confirm Dell is a registered NJTA vendor and map which IT categories run via direct public bidding versus reseller or any applicable NJ state cooperative.
Lead infrastructure (ISG) and edge/OT plays tied to cashless-tolling and ITS modernization — compute, storage, networking, and ruggedized edge for gantries and traffic operations.
Bring a cybersecurity and OT/IT-convergence narrative to CIO Jose Dios's organization as tolling and field systems go fully digital.
Time large proposals to NJTA's board calendar and bond issuances — contracts above $100K need Board of Commissioners authorization.
Build the executive relationship with ED Kris Kolluri and CFO Erwin Luna, and watch for shared technology priorities across NJTA and NJ TRANSIT.
